item.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

item.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

item.title: Google’s Pixel event is set for August 12
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Google's upcoming event in August will introduce new Pixel devices.

item.title: Figma acquires team behind a vibe-coding app
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The Y Combinator-backed company started a vibe-coding platform and later built an agent-creation product.

item.title: Netflix dabbles in shorter video content with its new set of publisher deals with Variety, others
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Netflix is bringing 2- to 20-minute videos to its platform through new partnerships with digital publishers, including Rolling Stone and Variety.

item.title: Hacked, leaked, and held for ransom: The worst breaches of 2026 so far
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: From a massive DOGE data breach and the hacking of critical energy and water systems to the hack of an FBI surveillance system, here are the most damaging security incidents and data breaches of 2026.

item.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

item.title: X adds a video editor to encourage creators to post original content, not stolen reposts
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X is rolling out a new video editor and recorder for iOS with multilingual captions, green-screen effects, and other editing tools.

item.title: VC firm Chemistry is raising $500M for its second fund
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Chemistry Ventures, the VC firm launched by Bessemer, Index Ventures, and Andreessen Horowitz alums, is raising $500M for its second fund.

item.title: AI law startup Norm raises $120M, hits unicorn valuation
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: AI law startup Norm has raised a $120 million Series C round led by Khosla Ventures, valuing the startup at $1.2 billion.

item.title: This startup pits dealerships against each other to bid on your used car
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Bidbus, which lets dealerships bid on used cars, has raised $15 million in a Series A round that was led by early-stage mobility fund Ibex Investors.

item.title: Hacktivists call out Trump by hacking and defacing US Army websites
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The U.S. Army has fixed two of its websites that were hacked to display messages calling President Trump a "pedophile" and a "thief."

item.title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.

item.title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.

item.title: Netflix invented binge-watching. Now it may have outgrown it.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: A new report suggests Netflix viewers aren’t sticking around for Season 2. The bigger issue may be that binge-watching itself is no longer the advantage it once was.

item.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.

item.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

item.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.

item.title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.

item.title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Connie Loizos
item.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.
